Let me start by reading the id cards for the specialist agents, understanding the project structure, and then proceeding with the workflow. Let me first read the id cards and then save the user brief.

id_cards.name: mcu_factory_requirements
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: mcu_factory_requirements
Role: Parse the user brief into requirements/spec/padframe requirements and soc_config, then run the exact custom spec advisory.
Strengths: Requirements extraction, soc_config authoring, ambiguity handling with questionnaire, advisory execution and traceability.
Handoffs: Receives project root and user brief path; returns docs + config + advisory output for downstream planning.
Limitations: Does not run nc_chipgen generation, verification, or PnR.

id_cards.name: mcu_factory_verification_planning
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: mcu_factory_verification_planning
Role: Build a complete verification checklist and generate all scenario YAMLs required by that checklist.
Strengths: Requirement-to-test mapping, scenario planning, checklist/dashboard generation.
Handoffs: Receives requirements/spec/soc_config and returns checklist markdown/html, scenario YAML set, and manifest mapping checklist IDs to scenarios.
Limitations: Planning-only stage; does not execute simulation commands.

id_cards.name: mcu_factory_generation
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: mcu_factory_generation
Role: Execute nc_chipgen generation with all planned scenarios using project-derived dynamic paths.
Strengths: Command construction, generation execution, output validation.
Handoffs: Receives project paths and scenario manifest; returns generation evidence and design root.
Limitations: Does not run verification or review.

id_cards.name: mcu_factory_verification
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: mcu_factory_verification
Role: Execute nc_chipgen verification (`--all --coverage`) and produce evidence-backed verification outputs.
Strengths: Verify command execution, artifact inspection, verification reporting.
Handoffs: Receives design root and paths; returns verification report, summary, and execution evidence.
Limitations: Does not author checklist planning and does not run final review advisory.

id_cards.name: mcu_factory_verification_reviewer
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: mcu_factory_verification_reviewer
Role: Review verification evidence, update checklist/dashboard, and run the exact custom verification review advisory.
Strengths: Evidence auditing, checklist status updates, dashboard reporting, advisory execution.
Handoffs: Receives verification artifacts and checklist; returns evaluation report, updated checklist/dashboard, and advisory output.
Limitations: Does not run generation/PnR and does not execute core verification tests.

id_cards.name: chipgen_pnr
id_cards.content:
Agent Name: chipgen_pnr
Role: Executes hierarchical Place-and-Route for the chipgen workflow — hardens the wrapper macro using nc_chipgen's pre-generated OpenLane config, then runs top-level chip PnR integrating the wrapper with the padframe GDS.
Strengths: OpenLane/LibreLane execution, hierarchical PnR strategy, padframe-driven floorplanning, timing closure, DRC/LVS signoff, GDS generation
Handoffs: Receives project path, MCU dir, pre-generated openlane config, core.def, padframe.gds → produces wrapper macro GDS/LEF and chip top GDS with timing/DRC/LVS reports
Limitations: Does not modify RTL (escalates back if needed). Does not re-run nc_chipgen. Uses nc_chipgen's pre-generated OpenLane configs for the wrapper — does not recreate from scratch.
